title: JSON type: string slice operation results in result value with JSON type, expected VARCHAR
description:
### What happens?

I accidentally stumbled upon this. I'm not even sure if it's a bug perse.
The issue is that applying a string slice operation on a value of the JSON type succeeds, and returns a value of the JSON type. 

This is different from the behavior of applying an equivalent substring function, which returns the VARCHAR type.


### To Reproduce

```
select '{"a":1}'::JSON as v_json
, v_json[2:3] as v_json_slice
, typeof(v_json_slice) as vjson_slice_type
, substring(v_json, 2, 2) as v_json_substr
, typeof(v_json_substr) as vjson_substr_type
;
```
result
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ v_json  в”Ӯ v_json_slice в”Ӯ vjson_slice_type в”Ӯ v_json_substr в”Ӯ vjson_substr_type в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  json   в”Ӯ     json     в”Ӯ     varchar      в”Ӯ    varchar    в”Ӯ      varchar      в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
```

key_bug_features: String slice operation on JSON type returns JSON type
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_json_data (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, data JSON NOT NULL);
SELECT data[2:3] AS json_slice, typeof(data[2:3]) AS slice_type FROM test_json_data WHERE id = 1;
SELECT substring(data::VARCHAR, 2, 2) AS json_substr, typeof(substring(data::VARCHAR, 2, 2)) AS substr_type FROM test_json_data WHERE id = 2;
SELECT id, data, data[2:3] AS json_slice, substring(data::VARCHAR, 2, 2) AS json_substr FROM test_json_data WHERE data IS NOT NULL;
